title: Streams, files and parsing

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific C++: Beginner to Intermediate draft for Streams, files and parsing; human standards, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: C++ streams carry formatting and error state; extraction must be checked before values are used, and line-oriented input must account for delimiters left by formatted extraction.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: An integer extraction appears directly in an if condition, and invalid input clears or terminates processing before the destination is trusted. Your goal is to implement file-backed input and output with explicit failure handling. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Conceptual constraints and diagnostics; Exceptions and guarantees; string, array and vector. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: C++ streams carry formatting and error state; extraction must be checked before values are used, and line-oriented input must account for delimiters left by formatted extraction. This rule is applied to concrete C++ source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Locale, buffering, synchronization, fail/eof distinctions, exceptions masks, and mixing formatted with line input affect behavior. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: istream extraction always assigns a usable default value and eof alone identifies every input failure.

includes: Use stream state and structured parsing without silently accepting malformed input.
Implement file-backed input and output with explicit failure handling.
excludes: Locale, buffering, synchronization, fail/eof distinctions, exceptions masks, and mixing formatted with line input affect behavior.

outcomes: Implement file-backed input and output with explicit failure handling.

examples.title: Streams, files and parsing: defined case
examples.situation: An integer extraction appears directly in an if condition, and invalid input clears or terminates processing before the destination is trusted.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the concrete source or state: An integer extraction appears directly in an if condition, and invalid input clears or terminates processing before the destination is trusted.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the conclusion: Check stream state after each required extraction and distinguish parse failure, EOF, and I/O error before using destination values.
examples.result: The defined result follows because Check stream state after each required extraction and distinguish parse failure, EOF, and I/O error before using destination values.

examples.title: Streams, files and parsing: boundary case
examples.situation: After reading a number, the program consumes the pending newline before getline reads a full name.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: After reading a number, the program consumes the pending newline before getline reads a full name.
Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: Locale, buffering, synchronization, fail/eof distinctions, exceptions masks, and mixing formatted with line input affect behavior.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: istream extraction always assigns a usable default value and eof alone identifies every input failure.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Implement file-backed input and output with explicit failure handling.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: istream extraction always assigns a usable default value and eof alone identifies every input failure.
misconceptions.correction: Check stream state after each required extraction and distinguish parse failure, EOF, and I/O error before using destination values.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using concrete CPP rules, explain why this belief fails: istream extraction always assigns a usable default value and eof alone identifies every input failure.

analogy.example: A system of self-managing components can model Streams, files and parsing: each component protects an invariant and owns resources through typed interfaces and lifetime.
usefulFor: Connecting types, value semantics, invariants, generic interfaces, ownership, and deterministic cleanup
limitations: C++ overload resolution, templates, value categories, object lifetime, and undefined behavior have no exact physical equivalent.
Locale, buffering, synchronization, fail/eof distinctions, exceptions masks, and mixing formatted with line input affect behavior.
